landing.heroDesc: Give your child a head start by teaching them to use AI with reason and creativity. Spark is built on the AI Fluency methodology — developed in collaboration with top universities and experts at Anthropic — offering a closed, safe environment where you have full oversight.
landing.frameworkTitle: The 4D Framework — the core of Spark
landing.frameworkDesc: Developed by Prof. Rick Dakan and Prof. Joseph Feller in collaboration with Anthropic — built on research analysing over 9,800 AI conversations. Four skills that empower your child to use AI with confidence and understanding.
landing.frameworkD1Desc: When should you use AI and when shouldn't you? Your child learns to divide tasks between themselves and the machine.
landing.frameworkD2Desc: How do I talk to AI? Clear instructions get better answers — your child learns the art.
landing.frameworkD3Desc: Is this right? Your child learns to evaluate AI responses and tell truth from nonsense.
landing.frameworkD4Desc: What am I responsible for? Your child learns about ethics, honesty, and using technology responsibly.
landing.pricingDesc: Choose the plan that fits your family best. No hidden fees, cancel anytime.
landing.pricingCardFreeDesc: Everything one child needs to master AI.
landing.pricingCardProDesc: Everything you need for multiple children.
landing.blogHookDesc: See how our 4D Framework (AI Fluency) is designed to build critical thinking and digital literacy in children through play.
landing.faq1Q: Is Spark safe for my child?
landing.faq1A: Yes, 100%. Spark uses strict Google Safety Settings. We never store personally identifiable information about children, and none of our AI models are trained on user data.
landing.faq2A: It's a methodology built on four pillars: Delegation, Description, Discernment and Diligence. The framework was developed by Prof. Rick Dakan and Prof. Joseph Feller in collaboration with Anthropic, based on research analysing over 9,800 AI conversations.
landing.faq3A: Spark is best suited for children aged 10–14. The missions require reading comprehension and critical thinking that perfectly suits this age group.
landing.faq4Q: Doesn't this just increase my child's 'screen time'?
landing.faq4A: No. All missions are designed as 'active screen time' where the child has to solve puzzles, reflect, and create. Furthermore, you receive Conversation Starters as a parent to take the discussion off the screen and into the living room.
landing.faq5Q: Can my child chat freely about anything with the AI?
landing.faq5A: No, the AI is strictly constrained (prompt engineered) to its role in each mission (e.g., answering as Queen Elizabeth I or a Martian robot). It cannot answer questions outside that framework.
landing.faq6Q: Do I need to know anything about AI as a parent?
landing.faq6A: Not at all. The parent dashboard is designed to explain exactly what your child is learning in each mission and gives you ideas on how to support them without needing to be a computer scientist.
landing.faq7Q: Does Spark work on all devices?
landing.faq7A: Yes, Spark is a Web App and works seamlessly on phones, tablets (iPads), and computers. As long as the device has a browser and an internet connection, your child can play.
landing.faq8Q: How does the subscription work and can I cancel anytime?
landing.faq8A: The subscription is billed monthly with no lock-in contract. If you want to take a break or cancel, you simply do so inside the parent dashboard with one click — no questions asked.
landing.footerMission: We prepare children for the future by fostering critical thinking and digital literacy through play and discovery.
landing.articlesSubtitle: Learn how AI is changing education and how you can support your child's learning journey.
landing.articlesHookDesc: We publish in-depth articles on safety, prompt engineering, and critical thinking in the age of AI.

methodology.desc: We don't just teach children to use AI — we teach them to understand it, steer it, and think critically about its output.
methodology.researchCredit: Developed by Prof. Rick Dakan and Prof. Joseph Feller in collaboration with Anthropic — built on research analysing over 9,800 AI conversations.
methodology.d1Desc: What should AI do and what should I do myself? Your child learns to delegate wisely — the machine helps, but the child leads.
methodology.d2Desc: How should I word my instructions? Clear language gets better answers. Your child practises the art of talking to technology.
methodology.d3Desc: Is this right or wrong? Your child learns to spot errors, bias, and evaluate whether the AI is telling the truth.
methodology.d4Desc: What am I responsible for? The child has the final word — they review, refine, and make the work their own. No copying, only original work.
methodology.safeDesc: Spark is built on the principle that your child's safety always comes first.
methodology.safe1Desc: Your child's data is never used to train external models or sold to third parties. Everything stays on our servers.
methodology.safe2Desc: Children don't get the answers — they learn to ask the right questions and build deeper understanding.
methodology.safe3Desc: You see all your child's progress, what they've learned, and get conversation starters for home.
methodology.ctaDesc: Register your child today and see how the 4D framework turns curiosity into real skills.

library.title: Concept Library
library.subtitle: Everything you've learned on your journey

why.title: Your children are already using AI.
why.subtitle: Give them the wisdom to do it well.
why.parentBody: You can't stop it — but you can make sure your child rises to the challenge. Children who understand AI will have an enormous advantage over those who simply use it without thinking.
why.childBody: Spark teaches children to ask better questions, think critically about AI responses, and make responsible decisions — while playing and discovering for themselves.

credibility.authors: Prof. Rick Dakan & Prof. Joseph Feller
